title: Surgeon
description: Surgeons are medical doctors who specialize in performing operations to treat injuries, diseases, and deformities. They diagnose and treat patients through surgical procedures, often working in hospitals or private practices.
content: Surgeons require extensive medical training, including an MBBS degree followed by postgraduate specialization (MS or DNB) in surgery. They must possess excellent hand-eye coordination, problem-solving skills, and the ability to work under pressure. Specializations include general surgery, neurosurgery, cardiac surgery, orthopedic surgery, and more. Continuous learning and adaptation to new surgical techniques are crucial.

financialFeasibility.note: Medical education is expensive in India. While government medical colleges offer subsidized fees, private medical colleges can be very costly. Loan options and scholarships are available, but financial planning is essential.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private hospitals, clinics, and private practice. Private sector often offers higher salaries, but government jobs provide job security and opportunities for public service.

courses: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.reason: Shares foundational medical knowledge and patient care skills.

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, the field is seeing increasing female representation, though challenges related to work-life balance persist.
